2606.20179.title: ReNikud: Audio-Supervised Hebrew Grapheme-to-Phoneme Conversion
2606.20179.paper_summary: Grapheme-to-phoneme (G2P) conversion for Modern Hebrew is needed for applications like text-to-speech (TTS), but is challenging due to the language's abjad writing system, which leaves vowels largely unwritten, creating substantial ambiguity. Standard approaches first predict vowel diacritics (nikud) to produce International Phonetic Alphabet (IPA) transcriptions, but this is limited: vocalization data is scarce and laborious to produce, it does not specify features such as lexical stress, and it reflects formal grammatical rules rather than everyday spoken pronunciation. Direct sequence-to-sequence IPA prediction, meanwhile, struggles on limited data and fails to exploit the character-level alignment characteristic of abjads. Our method, ReNikud, overcomes these limitations with two key insights: (1) Weak audio supervision via a phoneme-based automatic speech recognition (ASR) pseudo-labeling pipeline on thousands of hours of unlabeled Hebrew audio, yielding phonemic transcriptions that reflect natural spoken norms without manual annotation. (2) A pseudo-vocalization architecture that predicts IPA phonemes at each character position, enforcing character-level alignment as an inductive bias. Results on existing Hebrew G2P benchmarks and the new targeted MILIM benchmark for spoken Hebrew show that ReNikud surpasses previous state-of-the-art methods. We will release our code and trained models to support further work on Hebrew TTS and speech technologies.
2606.20179.paper_summary_zh: 字母到音素（G2P）轉換對於現代希伯來語的應用，如文本到語音（TTS），是必要的，但由於該語言的輕母音書寫系統，這一過程充滿挑戰，因為母音大多未被書寫，造成了相當大的歧義。標準方法首先預測母音標記（nikud），以生成國際音標（IPA）轉錄，但這是有限的：語音化數據稀缺且生成繁瑣，無法指定詞彙重音等特徵，並且反映的是正式語法規則，而非日常口語發音。與此同時，直接的序列到序列IPA預測在有限數據上表現不佳，並未利用輕母音特有的字符級對齊特性。我們的方法ReNikud通過兩個關鍵洞察克服了這些限制：（1）通過基於音素的自動語音識別（ASR）偽標記管道，對數千小時未標記的希伯來語音頻進行弱音頻監督，產生反映自然口語規範的音素轉錄，無需手動標註。（2）一種偽語音化架構，在每個字符位置預測IPA音素，強制字符級對齊作為歸納偏差。在現有的希伯來語G2P基準和針對口語希伯來語的新目標MILIM基準上的結果顯示，ReNikud超越了以往的最先進方法。我們將發布我們的代碼和訓練模型，以支持對希伯來語TTS和語音技術的進一步研究。
2606.20179.author: Maxim Melichov et.al.
2606.20179.authors: Maxim Melichov, Yakov Kolani, Morris Alper
